title: NBA Insider: Celtics, Heat are 2 teams ‘believed to intrigue’ Giannis in a blockbuster trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo is linked to trade rumors involving the Boston Celtics and Miami Heat.
The Celtics might need to include Jaylen Brown in a three-team trade to acquire Giannis.
Atlanta, Houston, and Portland have shown interest in Jaylen Brown for potential trade scenarios.

title: Read what Bruce Springsteen had to say about Trump, ICE, and America in Boston Sunday
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen criticized former President Trump and his administration during a concert at TD Garden in Boston.
Springsteen highlighted issues like the Voting Rights Act rollback, ICE actions, and the influence of a corrupt White House.
He called on Americans to actively defend their country's ideals and fight for a hopeful future.

title: Review and setlist: Springsteen eviscerates Trump at fiery TD Garden stop
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen criticized former President Trump during his TD Garden concert in Boston on May 24, 2026.
The concert featured 27 songs, emphasizing themes of political resistance and American ideals, with support from Tom Morello.
Springsteen performed a mix of classic hits and newer tracks, delivering an urgent and politically charged show.

title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22, marking his second collaboration with the orchestra.
The performance included a medley of American classics, jazz, and Broadway hits, featuring pieces by Copland, Ellington, and Rodgers.
Odom Jr. delivered notable performances of 'Wait for It' from Hamilton and Sam Cooke's 'A Change is Gonna Come.'

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, from Chelsea, is charged with multiple assault-related offenses involving two children under 4 years old.
Incidents include burning a child with a cigarette lighter and another with a heated fork, according to prosecutors.
After evidence surfaced, Bautista-Zepeda was apprehended in Virginia and awaits a dangerousness hearing on May 28.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, was convicted of rape and indecent assault in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023.
The victim, a 20-year-old woman, was assaulted in the hallway and restroom of the restaurant.
Jammal was sentenced to 13.5 to 15 years in prison, followed by 5 years of probation.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Church of the Open Word in Newtonville, built in 1893, will be converted into six condominiums.
A new five-story building with 26 condos will be constructed on the church property by developer Habitech Communities.
The church, once affiliated with the Swedenborgian Church, was purchased for $4 million in 2024 after closing in 2020.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard is a potential trade candidate for the Celtics, fitting well due to his versatility and defensive skills.
LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent, could be a suitable fit in Boston's lineup if he leaves LA.
John Collins offers forward depth for the Celtics with a history of strong offensive performance despite a less stellar recent season.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series for the first time, defeating Molloy University in a three-game series.
Endicott College reached the NCAA Division III championship round for the fourth consecutive year with a win over Salve Regina University.
Three Endicott players hit home runs in Game 3, securing a 12-4 victory and advancing to the College World Series in Ohio.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Celtics plan to work out UConn's Alex Karaban, ranked No. 32 on ESPN’s top 100 draft prospects list.
Boston holds the No. 27 and No. 40 picks in the 2026 NBA Draft, scheduled for next month.
Karaban, a Southborough native, won two NCAA titles at UConn and ranks sixth in program scoring history.
